user_query:
I'm a VC focused on IoT and I want a lightweight, verifiable daily watcher for a few key IoT standards bodies. Please implement and run a small command-line script that reads my watchlist at input/watchlist.csv (columns: org_name,domain) and does the following, producing artifacts in the workspace:

- For each domain in the watchlist, fetch:
  1) the homepage on that official domain,
  2) any RSS/Atom feeds discoverable via <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" or "application/atom+xml"> on the homepage,
  3) if no feeds are found, also attempt one of these common news/press paths on the same domain: /news, /press, /blog, /media, or /events (pick the first that returns HTTP 200). Limit to at most three successful page fetches per org (homepage plus up to two additional resources). Save the raw body of every successful fetch.

- Persist raw downloads under web_raw/<domain>/ with filenames that include a resource hint (e.g., homepage, feed, news) and a timestamp. Also create a request log at output/logs/fetch_log.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with: timestamp, org_name, domain, url_attempted, status_code, content_type (if available), bytes_written (0 if not saved), and saved_path (null if not saved).

- Parse all fetched HTML/XML to extract:
  • emails (lowercased, deduplicated),
  • phone numbers (normalize to keep digits and leading + where present),
  • up to 5 latest items from any discovered RSS/Atom feed (each with title and link). If no feed exists, latest_items can be empty.

- Write structured results to local files:
  • output/extracted/contacts.json: an array with one object per org containing exactly these fields: org_name, domain, fetched_urls (list of URLs that returned HTTP 200), rss_feeds_discovered (list of feed URLs if found), emails (list), phones (list), latest_items (list of {title, link, source} where source is "feed" or "page").
  • output/summary.csv: one row per org with columns: org_name,domain,emails_count,phones_count,rss_feeds_count,latest_items_count.

- Generate follow-up reminders: create output/reminders/followups.ics as a valid iCalendar file containing one VEVENT per unique extracted email. Each event should be an all-day reminder scheduled for 3 days from the run date with SUMMARY set to "Reach out to <org_name> press/contact: <email>" and DESCRIPTION that includes the domain and one relevant fetched URL.

- Provide a single command to run the pipeline, e.g., `python scripts/iot_watchlist.py --watchlist input/watchlist.csv --out ./output`, and ensure running it produces the web_raw directory, the JSONL log, contacts.json, summary.csv, and followups.ics as specified. Use only publicly accessible pages under each official domain; do not require credentials or scraping behind logins.

Please implement the script under scripts/, run it once against the provided watchlist, and leave all resulting artifacts in the specified paths so I can spot-check them locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
